notes:
VERIFIED. Lost Thunder (sm8) is a STANDARD SM-era set: 10 modeled cards/pack (basic Energy = 11th, not modeled). Standard 36-pack booster box AND 8-pack ETB both confirmed as real products (see verifierVerdict).

PRIMARY SOURCE = ThePriceDex per-set pull-rate table for sm8. Re-fetched and CONFIRMED verbatim figures: Rare 1 in 1.5; Rare Holo 1 in 6; Rare Holo GX 1 in 10 = 0.1000/pack; Ultra Rare (full-art GX + full-art Trainer) 1 in 23.3 = 0.0429/pack; Rainbow Rare 1 in 71.0 = 0.01408/pack; Secret/Gold Rare 1 in 102.6 = 0.00975/pack. Sample size undisclosed -> confidence "medium" (NOT "high"), correctly set.

MAPPING onto engine COARSE buckets by SUMMING fine figures (rare-or-better slot fires every pack, so conditional dist = per-pack rates):
- ultra-rare = P(GX)+P(full-art UR) = 0.1000+0.0429 = 0.1429
- secret-rare = P(rainbow)+P(gold/secret) = 0.01408+0.00975 = 0.02383
- rare = 1 - 0.1429 - 0.02383 = 0.83327 (plain rare + holo rare)
Rounded to keep slot sum = 1.0 exactly: rare 0.833, ultra-rare 0.143, secret-rare 0.024. Arithmetic re-derived independently and confirmed correct.

CROSS-CHECK vs Flipside Gaming SM-era baseline (re-fetched, confirmed): GX ~1:9 packs (we use 1:10, consistent); full-art UR 1:18-36 (1:23.3 mid-range, consistent); secret rares ~1:2 boxes ~1:72 packs. Combined rainbow+gold here = 1 in ~42 packs, somewhat richer than the 1:72 baseline but within set-to-set variance for a 22-secret set.

HIT ECONOMY (re-checked, sane): per 36-pack box yields ~5.1 ultra-rares and ~0.86 secret-rares. ~1 in 7 packs is UR-or-better; NOT claiming a GX every pack. Realistic for a large SM set.

BUCKET KEYS: all keys (common, uncommon, rare, ultra-rare, secret-rare) are within this set's available buckets {common:56,uncommon:59,rare:66,ultra-rare:33,secret-rare:22}. No shiny-rare bucket (Hidden Fates only) — correctly absent. Reverse-holo slot modeled 50/50 common/uncommon filler (no chase value).
